pitch.title: Right Time, Wrong Place
pitch.core_theme: Learning to let go of the past to embrace the unexpected future.
pitch.logline: A cynical divorce attorney discovers a magical photobooth that sends her back to the exact moments her past relationships fell apart, but she keeps running into the same charming stranger in every timeline.
pitch.synopsis: Act 1: Annie, a successful but lonely divorce attorney, gets drunk at a wedding and uses an antique photobooth, wishing she could fix her past mistakes. She wakes up five years earlier on the day of her worst breakup. Act 2: As Annie jumps through different pivotal relationship failures, she realizes she cannot change the outcomes. However, a quirky wedding photographer named Jack keeps appearing in these timelines. They bond over their mutual displacement in time and start falling for each other. Act 3: Annie must make a choice: stay in a perfect, curated past with Jack or return to her present reality, risking everything on the hope that she can find the real Jack in the modern day. She chooses the present, finds Jack, and they begin a new, unwritten chapter together.

stage2_outline.title: Right Time, Wrong Place
stage2_outline.logline: A cynical divorce attorney trapped in a haunted mansion discovers a cursed photobooth that forces her to survive terrifying, demonic manifestations of her past breakups, aided only by a mysterious ghost hunter trapped in the same nightmare.

beats.description: Annie dominates divorce court by proving love is a toxic trap, therefore she begrudgingly attends her sister's wedding at a notoriously haunted mansion and avoids all romantic encounters.

Annie gets drunk and wanders into the mansion's shadowy basement to hide, therefore she steps inside a rotting antique photobooth and wishes to erase her past, but a blood-red flash plunges her into a nightmarish, supernatural version of the house.

Annie wakes up trapped in a twisted, decaying version of the mansion on the day of her worst breakup, therefore she tries to reason with the manifestation of her ex, but he transforms into a grotesque entity that hunts her through the dark corridors.

Annie realizes the timeline will reset only if she uses a past version of the cursed photobooth, therefore she takes another picture while fleeing the monster, firmly locking herself into a horrifying labyrinth of her past romantic failures.

Annie lands in a new, terrifying timeline facing a demonic manifestation of a different ex, therefore she tries to hide in the mansion's attic, but she spots a bloodied paranormal photographer named Jack hiding in the shadows.
Annie corners Jack, therefore they realize they are both trapped in this shifting, malevolent purgatory, but neither knows how to break the curse before the house consumes them.

Jack agrees to help Annie navigate the deadly traps of her manifested traumas, therefore they construct makeshift weapons to fight off her monstrous exes, but every victory only makes the haunted house more hostile and structurally unstable.

Annie stops running from her past and fights back alongside Jack to secure a safe room, therefore they share a profound, adrenaline-fueled romantic connection, fundamentally shifting her goal from mere survival to saving them both from the house.

Annie and Jack discover a brief moment of peace and fall deeply in love, therefore they mistakenly believe they can survive indefinitely in the shadows, but the cursed photobooth strip physically manifests in blood to show they only have one jump left before their souls are trapped forever.
Jack reveals he is a spirit bound to the walls of this house, therefore he begs Annie to use the final jump to escape alone, but Annie refuses to abandon him to the dark entities closing in.

The haunted mansion unleashes a massive, horrific amalgamation of all Annie's past traumas to destroy them, therefore she pleads with Jack to step into the booth with her, but the house physically restrains him.

Annie makes the agonizing choice to break the curse by destroying the booth from the inside, therefore she triggers the final flash as the monsters tear into the room, sacrificing her safety for a terrifying leap into the unknown.
Annie wakes up gasping in the present day inside the dusty, dormant photobooth at her sister's wedding, therefore she rushes out to find Jack, but the basement is empty and completely normal.

Annie uses her aggressive investigative skills to research the mansion's history, therefore she discovers Jack was a missing photographer who recently escaped the house's curse and woke up from a coma, but she is terrified his trauma erased his memory of her.

Annie tracks Jack down to a local hospital where he is recovering, but he initially looks at her as a stranger, therefore she takes a massive leap of faith and shows him a torn photo strip from the cursed booth.

Jack sees the photo and feels a strange, undeniable spark of recognition, therefore Annie finally lets go of her past fears, stepping confidently into a completely unwritten, ghost-free future together.
